lines.text: Good evening. [pause] It's {{CLOCK}} in Madison, and you are inside. [pause] That is enough. That has always been enough.
We'll begin where we always begin. [pause] Breathe in through the nose for four counts. [pause] Hold. [pause] And release through the mouth. [pause] Again.
Let your shoulders drop. [pause] Let your jaw unclench. You've been holding it — I know. You've been holding it since seven o'clock. [pause] You can set it down now. Not all of it. Just the jaw.
Breathe in. [pause] Hold for four. [pause] Release. [pause] Now I want you to picture your hands. Palms up. Open. [pause] You are not holding a weapon. You chose that. [pause] Breathe in again.
There is a sound outside your window. [pause] You already know what it is. You do not need to look. [pause] Your eyes are closed. Your door is closed. These are the same decision. [pause] Breathe.

The Sisters teach us that stillness is not passivity. [pause] Stillness is the loudest thing a body can do on a night like this. [pause] You are screaming right now and you don't even know it. [pause] Good.

Breathe in. [pause] Hold. [pause] Now think of someone you haven't spoken to in a long time. [pause] Not someone you miss. Someone you stopped choosing. [pause] Hold them in your mind. [pause] You don't have to forgive them. You just have to notice that you remembered.
Release. [pause] We are {{ELAPSED}} into the night. [pause] You have survived every minute of it so far. That is not luck. That is you, deciding, sixty times a minute, to stay.

Breathe in. [pause] Picture your front door. You know what it looks like from the inside — the deadbolt, the chain, the light underneath. [pause] Now picture what's on the other side. [pause] Now stop. [pause] You don't need to see it. You chose the side you're on. Breathe out.

There is a woman on Williamson Street tonight — I don't know her, I've never met her — who is sitting in a bathtub with her children because the bathtub is the room with no windows. [pause] She is breathing. [pause] I'd like to breathe with her now. [pause] In. [pause] Out. [pause] In.

The dawn comes at the same time whether you watch for it or not. [pause] You do not need to earn the morning. The morning is not a reward. [pause] It is just the next thing that happens. [pause] Let your hands rest. Let your eyes stay closed. I'll be here.
Breathe in. [pause] Hold for four. [pause] Release. [pause] You are still here. [pause] Stay.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide confirmed kills at {{CLOCK}}: tracking above 2168 pace through hour 5 — last year's cumulative at this point was 61
State Street ruins activity escalating — coordinated movement reported near the 500 block — residents in the area are advised to remain sheltered
"Stillness is the loudest thing a body can do." — Sister Marigold, PRGE wellness hour
Text from listener in Vilas Park: "Marigold got me through last year. Breathing with you again. Thank you."
